scope_summary: Sets out the principles and requirements the WAPC uses to assess and determine statements of grounds and community development statements under the Community Titles Act 2018, and the criteria for subsequent subdivision and development within community schemes. It applies statewide to freehold land proposed to be subdivided as a community (titles) scheme of up to three tiers, and explains when an approved community development statement is binding on, or must be given due regard in, subdivision and development decisions. Procedural timeframes, consultation, amendment, right of review and cessation provisions are included.

cross_references.cited: Caravan Park and Camping Grounds Act 1995
State Planning Policy 1 State Planning Framework
Development Control Policy 2.3 Public Open Space in Residential Areas

quality_flags: document is styled 'Operational Policy 1.11', not 'Development Control Policy 1.11' as the manifest instrument_name says (same instrument, re-badged by WAPC)
publication/operational/last-reviewed dates all June 2021 per imprint page
imprint/disclaimer block extracted as heavily garbled interleaved text on page 1

procedural timeframes (4-year approval period, 10-year development period, 28/42-day referrals, 120-day WAPC assessment) are process rules, not development standards, so key_numeric_standards left empty
